titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to Ellen Gleeson, 9 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Patrick Carey to Ellen Gleeson informing her that there is now a vacancy for her in Richmond Hospital, Dublin and if she doesn't attend, she will miss it.
Patrick Carey (1853-1934) was the Union Clerk for the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians. Poor law unions and their workhouses were established by the Poor Law (Ireland) Act, 1838, as a response to widespread poverty, and were each governed by a Board of Guardians. This letter comes from the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians Minute Book.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9th Decr 1915 Dear Madam, There is no w a vacancy for you in the Richmond
Hospital, Dublin. You had better attend here at once
otherwise the vacancy might be filled:
Yours faithfully,
Clerk of Union. Mrs EllenGleeson,
Ballydedder,
Beaufort.
